title: Cheilanthes rosei
intro: Cheilanthes rosei, also known as the rose mullein or rose rock fern, is a species of fern in the family Polypodiaceae. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, and it has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including inflammation and fever, due to its potent anti-inflammatory and antipyretic effects. The bioactive constituents of Cheilanthes rosei include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Cheilanthes rosei is commonly prepared as a tea or infused in oil to create a topical salve, and it is also used as a component in some herbal remedies, such as anti-inflammatory creams and ointments.
taxonomy: Cheilanthes rosei is classified within the family Pteridaceae, commonly known as the maidenhair fern family. This species belongs to the genus Cheilanthes, which includes ferns often referred to as lip ferns. The classification of Cheilanthes rosei is further divided into the order Polypodiales and the class Polypodiopsida. Within the kingdom Plantae, Cheilanthes rosei is a member of the phylum Pteridophyta, which includes vascular plants that reproduce via spores. The specific classification of Cheilanthes rosei is as follows: Kingdom: Plantae, Phylum: Pteridophyta, Class: Polypodiopsida, Order: Polypodiales, Family: Pteridaceae, Genus: Cheilanthes, Species: C. rosei.
